analyses.name: NVIDIA Corporation
analyses.verdict: The infrastructure layer of the AI buildout — still growing into a rich multiple rather than out of it.

financials.summary: A second straight year of step-change growth as Data Center became the overwhelming majority of revenue. Margins expanded to best-in-class levels on Hopper/Blackwell mix and pricing power, and the balance sheet turned into a cash machine — free cash flow more than covered record buybacks with room to spare.
highlights: Operating cash flow > $60B; net cash positive

accomplishments: Blackwell (B200/GB200) ramped to volume — the fastest product ramp in company history
Closed the year as one of the largest companies in the world by market cap
CUDA + networking (NVLink, Spectrum-X) deepened the full-stack moat beyond the GPU
Major cloud and sovereign-AI commitments booked into the forward pipeline

journey_52w.summary: A powerful uptrend punctuated by two sharp drawdowns — a sentiment shock early in the year on fears of cheaper training methods, and a mid-year consolidation — both of which were bought. The stock made a series of higher highs as each earnings report re-rated forward estimates upward.

milestones.label: Q1
milestones.note: Sharp drawdown on 'efficient training' fears, then recovery as hyperscaler capex guidance rose

milestones.label: Mid-year
milestones.note: Blackwell volume shipments confirmed; estimates revised higher

milestones.label: Recent
milestones.note: Reclaimed highs on continued data-center demand visibility

risks: Customer concentration — a handful of hyperscalers drive most Data Center revenue
Any sign of an AI-capex digestion phase would compress the multiple fast
Export-control / China policy is an ongoing overhang
catalysts: Sovereign-AI and enterprise demand broadening the customer base
watch: Hyperscaler capex revisions each earnings season
critical.valuation: Premium forward multiple, but supported so far by earnings revisions outrunning the price — the risk is multiple compression if growth normalizes.

analyses.name: Apple Inc.
analyses.verdict: A cash-generative compounder leaning on Services growth while the market waits for the next hardware cycle and a credible AI story.

financials.summary: Roughly flat-to-modest revenue growth with the mix continuing to shift toward high-margin Services, which lifted overall gross margin. Enormous free cash flow funded one of the largest buyback-plus-dividend programs in the market; the company again returned more cash than it generated, shrinking the share count.
highlights: Services at a record, ~25%+ of revenue and the main margin driver
Diluted EPS up on margin mix + buybacks

accomplishments: Services reached a new all-time revenue record
Installed base hit fresh highs, widening the recurring-revenue base
Continued silicon leadership across the product line

journey_52w.summary: A choppier year than the mega-cap peers — periods of underperformance on soft hardware growth and questions about its AI roadmap, offset by the steady Services narrative and relentless buybacks supporting the floor.

milestones.label: H1
milestones.note: Range-bound as iPhone unit growth stayed muted

milestones.label: H2
milestones.note: Services strength and capital return kept it resilient into year-end

risks: Hardware growth is mature — the story increasingly rests on Services
AI roadmap perceived as behind peers; execution here is the swing factor
Regulatory pressure on the App Store / Services economics
catalysts: A credible on-device AI feature cycle driving an upgrade super-cycle
watch: iPhone unit trajectory and China demand
critical.valuation: Trades at a premium to its growth rate — justified by quality and buybacks, but it leaves little room for disappointment.

analyses.name: ASML Holding N.V.
analyses.verdict: A genuine monopoly on EUV lithography — the single most essential supplier to the entire advanced-chip industry.

financials.summary: Revenue grew on a richer EUV mix and rising system ASPs, with net margins holding above 25%. The order book is the headline asset — a multi-year backlog that gives unusual visibility for a capital-equipment company, funded by customers who have no alternative supplier.
highlights: Revenue up YoY on EUV mix and ASP gains
Strong free cash flow funding dividend + buybacks
Sole global supplier of EUV lithography systems

accomplishments: Continued High-NA EUV shipments to leading-edge foundries
Backlog extended on AI-driven leading-edge capacity expansion
Maintained an effective 100% share of EUV

journey_52w.summary: Volatile but upward — swung on order-intake prints and China-export headlines, then trended to new highs as AI capex reaffirmed multi-year demand for leading-edge capacity.

milestones.label: Early
milestones.note: Pulled back on a soft order-intake quarter and China-policy headlines

milestones.label: Later
milestones.note: Rallied to highs as AI-capex commentary lifted forward bookings

risks: China export-control policy (China has been a meaningful share of sales)
Order intake is lumpy — a single soft quarter can spook the stock
catalysts: High-NA EUV adoption at 2nm and below
watch: Net bookings each quarter (the key tell)
China revenue mix and any new export restrictions
critical.valuation: Always trades at a premium to the equipment group; the monopoly justifies it, but bookings volatility makes the entry point matter.
